name: Google Classroom API
what_it_does: Google's REST API for managing classrooms, courses, assignments, student rosters, and grades within Google Workspace for Education.
use_cases: Automating course creation and roster management for schools
Building attendance and progress tracking dashboards
Syncing Google Classroom data with SIS (Student Information Systems)
Creating assignments from external content sources
not_for: Higher education with complex LMS requirements (use Canvas or Blackboard)
Consumer apps without Google Workspace for Education accounts
best_when: Your school or district uses Google Workspace for Education and you need to automate classroom administration or integrate with SIS/reporting tools.
avoid_when: You're not in the Google Workspace for Education ecosystem or need advanced LMS features like rubrics, peer review, or LTI integrations.

auth.notes: OAuth2 required. Domain-wide delegation with service accounts works for admin automation. User consent flows needed for student/teacher-context operations. Scopes are granular (read vs write per resource type).

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free for Google Workspace for Education users
pricing.paid_tiers: None for API; Google Workspace licensing required
pricing.notes: API is free but requires Google Workspace for Education account. Consumer Gmail accounts have limited Classroom API access. Rate limits: 500 requests/100 seconds per project.

performance.rate_limits: 500 req/100s per project, 10 req/s per user

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Standard Google API errors with code, message, and status fields. Domain-specific errors are reasonably descriptive.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Duplicate course or assignment creation results in new resources.
known_agent_gotchas: Domain-wide delegation setup requires Google Workspace admin — cannot be self-configured by agents
Student data is highly regulated (FERPA/COPPA) — log nothing about students
Courses must be in 'ACTIVE' state to accept assignments; agent must check state before submitting
Google Classroom is designed for K-12/higher ed workflows — limited customization compared to Canvas
Push notifications (webhooks) require Cloud Pub/Sub setup for real-time updates
Rate limits are per-project not per-key — shared projects can hit limits unexpectedly

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth2 with classroom-specific scopes. FERPA compliance required for student data. SOC2, FedRAMP. Student and teacher PII — educational data has strict legal protections (FERPA, COPPA). Domain-wide delegation for institutional use.
